title: Goal and time frame — same 24 hours as Bill Gates
html:
TL;DR

  • June 26, 2019 two-line aphorism: "Time is most valuable asset / Same 24 hours as Bill Gates" — the canonical hustle-culture "same 24 hours as Beyoncé"-style time-leverage talisman, with Gates as the comparator.
  • One of the earliest dated Bill Gates-as-mentor-from-afar invocations in the vault — three months before the "watch decoding bill gates" todo and four months before the self-mocking "hide behind Bill Gates books" reversal.
  • Reads as a pep talk as aphorism — typical of the summer-2019 pre-senior-year run-up.
  • Sits with the mentors-from-afar thread that later pulls in Son, Chamath, and Cuban.
  • Time-as-asset framing — early 10x thinking vocabulary surfacing without the label.

Key quote

"Time is most valuable asset. Same 24 hours as Bill Gates."

Why it matters in the graph

The note is 20 words but does double duty: it slots Bill Gates into the mentor-from-afar role, and it states the time as asset frame that the rest of the 2019 corpus operates inside. Yahir at 17 already has the model that the constraint is attention/time, not opportunity — a framing he'll restate hundreds of ways across the next seven years.

The Gates-cope sequence runs June (this aphorism) → September (watch the doc) → October (admit it's a cope) — a three-step arc of a teen mythologizing, then ironizing, his own role-models. By the time of the October note, Yahir's already self-critiquing the framing established here.
